description: Create and manage LinkedIn member and page posts

auth.usage_instructions: OAuth authentication management, live token verification, token refresh, logout, readiness checks, and nested profile management.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Run the LinkedIn OAuth authorization-code login flow. Use `--credential-type oauth_authorization_code` to target the OAuth credential explicitly, `--force/-F` to clear active token state before re-authenticating, and `--profile/-p` for a named profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing ephemeral auth state and re-authenticate

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored LinkedIn OAuth credentials for the selected profile. Use `--profile/-p` to target a named profile instead of the active default profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Manage named authentication profiles used by the LinkedIn CLI.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile named NAME from the LinkedIn .env template. Use this before configuring separate staging or alternate LinkedIn app credentials.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete the named profile and its stored data. Use --force/-F to skip the confirmation prompt.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get details for one saved auth profile by NAME. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all configured LinkedIn auth profiles and show which one is the default. Use --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --table/-t, and --properties/-p to shape the result.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

readiness.help: Show LinkedIn post command readiness by profile and scope.
readiness.usage_instructions: Show whether each LinkedIn auth profile is ready for member and page post command groups. Member writes require `w_member_social`, member reads require `r_member_social`, page writes require `w_organization_social`, and page reads require `r_organization_social`. Use `--profile/-p` for one profile and `--table/-t` for tabular output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check. Defaults to all profiles

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Refresh the stored LinkedIn OAuth access token using the saved refresh token. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile and --table/-t for formatted output.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.
status.usage_instructions: Check LinkedIn OAuth authentication status across profiles. The command performs live token verification and reports saved scopes, token status, and whether the current OAuth credentials are usable. Use `--profile/-p` to inspect one profile and `--table/-t` for tabular output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run the CLI authentication verification command across profiles. Use --profile/-p to scope the check, --verbose/-v for more detail, and --table/-t for tabular output.

cache.usage_instructions: Manage cached LinkedIn API responses stored for this CLI profile.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Remove every cached LinkedIn API response for the active profile. Use when you need fresh server results instead of cached reads.

page.usage_instructions: LinkedIn page operations. Use `linkedin page posts <action>` for official API organization or showcase-page post management.

posts.usage_instructions: Page-post operations through LinkedIn's official Posts API: list, get, create, update, delete, and client-side search. Page writes require `w_organization_social`; page reads require `r_organization_social`. Use `--page` or `LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN` to select the organization or showcase-page author.

create.help: Create a text-only LinkedIn page post.
create.usage_instructions: Create a text-only organization or showcase-page post from `COMMENTARY` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Pass `--page` or configure `LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN` to choose the page author. Use `--visibility`, `--feed-distribution`, and `--disable-reshare` to control delivery. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --page
options.help: Page author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN when set

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a page post by `POST_URN` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Pass `--page` or configure `LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN` so scope errors can be attributed correctly. Use `--table/-t` for formatted output and `--profile` for a named auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --page
options.help: Page author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN when set

get.help: Get a single page post by URN.
get.usage_instructions: Get one page post by `POST_URN` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Pass `--page` with the page URN or rely on `LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN` when configured. Use `--view-context`, `--table/-t`, or `--properties/-p` to shape the output. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --page
options.help: Page author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN when set

list.help: List LinkedIn posts for an organization or showcase page.
list.usage_instructions: List organization or showcase-page posts through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Pass `--page` with the page URN or rely on `LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN` when configured. Use `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--table/-t`, and `--properties/-p` to shape the result. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --page
options.help: Page author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN when set

options.name: --sort-by
options.help: Sort order accepted by LinkedIn (for example LAST_MODIFIED)

search.help: Search page posts with client-side wildcard matching.
search.usage_instructions: Search organization or showcase-page posts with a wildcard `QUERY` across rows returned by LinkedIn's official Posts API. Pass `--page` with the page URN or rely on `LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN` when configured. Use `--limit/-l` to cap fetched rows, then refine with `--filter/-f`, `--table/-t`, or `--properties/-p`. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --page
options.help: Page author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN when set

update.help: Update the commentary for an existing page post.
update.usage_instructions: Update the commentary for an existing page post identified by `POST_URN` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Pass `--page` or configure `LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN` so scope errors can be attributed correctly. Use `--table/-t` for formatted output and `--profile` for a named auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --page
options.help: Page author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PAGE_URN when set

user.usage_instructions: Authenticated-member operations. Use `linkedin user posts <action>` for official API member-post list, get, create, update, delete, and search operations.

posts.usage_instructions: Member-post operations through LinkedIn's official Posts API: list, get, create, update, delete, and client-side search. Member writes require `w_member_social`; member reads require `r_member_social`. Use `--person` or `LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN` when authenticated-member identity lookup is unavailable.

create.help: Create a text-only LinkedIn member post.
create.usage_instructions: Create a text-only member post from `COMMENTARY` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Omit `--person` to use `LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN`, then authenticated-member identity lookup through `/v2/me` and `/v2/userinfo` when available. Use `--visibility`, `--feed-distribution`, and `--disable-reshare` to control delivery. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --person
options.help: Member author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN, then the authenticated member when LinkedIn identity lookup is available

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a member post by `POST_URN` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Use `--person` when the CLI cannot infer the member author from config or identity lookup so scope errors can be attributed correctly. Use `--table/-t` for formatted output and `--profile` for a named auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --person
options.help: Member author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN, then the authenticated member when identity lookup is available

get.help: Get a single member post by URN.
get.usage_instructions: Get one member post by `POST_URN` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Use `--person` when the CLI cannot infer the member author from config or identity lookup. Use `--view-context`, `--table/-t`, or `--properties/-p` to shape the output. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --person
options.help: Member author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN, then the authenticated member when identity lookup is available

list.help: List LinkedIn posts for a member author.
list.usage_instructions: List member posts from LinkedIn's official Posts API for the resolved member author. Use `--person` to target a specific member URN, otherwise the CLI falls back to `LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN`, then authenticated-member identity lookup. Use `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--table/-t`, and `--properties/-p` to shape the result. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --person
options.help: Member author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN, then the authenticated member when identity lookup is available

options.name: --sort-by
options.help: Sort order accepted by LinkedIn (for example LAST_MODIFIED)

search.help: Search member posts with client-side wildcard matching.
search.usage_instructions: Search member posts with a wildcard `QUERY` across rows returned by LinkedIn's official Posts API for the resolved member author. Use `--person` to target a specific member URN, otherwise the CLI falls back to `LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN`, then authenticated-member identity lookup. Use `--limit/-l` to cap fetched rows, then refine with `--filter/-f`, `--table/-t`, or `--properties/-p`. Use `--profile` to select a non-default LinkedIn auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --person
options.help: Member author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN, then the authenticated member when identity lookup is available

update.help: Update the commentary for an existing member post.
update.usage_instructions: Update the commentary for an existing member post identified by `POST_URN` through LinkedIn's official Posts API. Use `--person` when the CLI cannot infer the member author from config or identity lookup so scope errors can be attributed correctly. Use `--table/-t` for formatted output and `--profile` for a named auth profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Auth profile name. Defaults to the active profile

options.name: --person
options.help: Member author URN. Defaults to LINKEDIN_PERSON_URN, then the authenticated member when identity lookup is available

usage_instructions: Use for LinkedIn member-post, page-post, and OAuth authentication operations through the `linkedin` CLI. Use `linkedin user posts <action>` for member posts and `linkedin page posts <action>` for organization or showcase-page posts. All post commands use LinkedIn's official OAuth APIs only. Use `--profile` on post commands when selecting a non-default LinkedIn profile.
